result:
**Root cause:** `addquoted` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:1502` always emitted control bytes as 3-digit zero-padded escapes (`\NNN`). C-Lua's `lstrlib.c addquoted` only zero-pads when the *next* byte in the source string is an ASCII digit (to keep `\D` from being misparsed as a multi-digit numeric escape). For `"\0"` we produced `"\000"` instead of `"\0"`, so `assert(string.format('%q', "\0") == [["\0"]])` failed.

**Fix:** Replaced the always-3-digit branch with C-Lua's rule — peek `s[idx+1]`, use `\{:03}` when it's an ASCII digit, otherwise the minimal `\{}` form. Also dropped a stale `// TODO(port)` and the unused `next = 0u8` placeholder.

**New state:** Build green. Re-running the test now advances 16 lines further; failure has moved to combined line 219 (`assert(string.format("%%%d %010d", 10, 23) == "%10 0000000023")`) — a separate `%010d` zero-padding bug, not in scope for this turn. No temporary instrumentation was added, so nothing to remove.

tool_input.command: cargo build -q -p lua-cli 2>&1 | grep -E "^error" | head -5; echo "exit=$?"

head -195 reference/lua-c/testes/strings.lua > /tmp/strings_partial.lua
echo 'print("done up to 195")' >> /tmp/strings_partial.lua
target/debug/lua-rs "$(cat /tmp/strings_partial.lua)" 2>&1 | tail -20

awk 'NR<=195' reference/lua-c/testes/strings.lua > /tmp/strings_partial.lua; printf '\nprint("done up to 195")\n' >> /tmp/strings_partial.lua; target/debug/lua-rs "$(awk '{print}' /tmp/strings_partial.lua)" 2>&1 | tail -10
awk 'NR<=195 {print}; NR==195 {print "print(\"done up to 195\")"}' reference/lua-c/testes/strings.lua > /tmp/strings_partial.lua && wc -l /tmp/strings_partial.lua
awk 'NR<=195 {print}; NR==195 {print "print(\"done up to 195\")"}' reference/lua-c/testes/strings.lua > harness/impl/_strings_partial.lua && wc -l harness/impl/_strings_partial.lua
tool_input.description: Create partial test file in harness/impl/
Check exact content of combined file around line 203
